title: Converting a Reference to a Tenancy

content.intro: Once a reference is completed, you can convert it into a tenancy. This creates a formal tenancy record with all the tenant information pre-filled from the reference.

steps.title: Find the completed reference
steps.content: Go to References and click the 'Completed' tab to see all completed references.

steps.title: Click 'Convert to Tenancy'
steps.content: On the reference row, click the 'Convert to Tenancy' button. This opens the conversion modal.

steps.title: Review tenancy details
steps.content: The conversion modal shows all details from the reference. Review the property address, rent amount, move-in date, and tenant information.

steps.title: Make any adjustments
steps.content: If needed, you can adjust the move-in date, rent amount, or other details before converting.

steps.title: Confirm conversion
steps.content: Click 'Convert to Tenancy' to create the tenancy. The reference will be marked as converted and a new draft tenancy will be created.

sections.title: Draft Tenancies
sections.content: Newly converted tenancies start in 'Draft' status. You'll need to request initial monies, generate an agreement, and complete the move-in process to activate the tenancy.

sections.title: What Gets Copied
sections.content: The conversion copies all tenant details (names, contact info, dates of birth), guarantor details if applicable, property information, rent amount, and proposed move-in date. You don't need to re-enter any of this information.

sections.title: All Tenants Must Complete
sections.content: You can only convert a reference once all tenants (and any guarantors) have completed their reference forms. If some are still pending, you'll need to wait or chase them to complete.
